blanks.sentence: Those aged 18 to 30 feel _____ by politicians
Helps Portuguese youth reconnect with their _____
Topics range from repairing facilities to modifying the _____
Local politicians attend and _____ to ideas
Bernardo Goncalves started the initiative in his _____
Concerned about declining _____ in democratic institutions
Successfully replicated in Hungary, _____ and Mozambique

multipleChoice.question: What is Neil’s opinion about what makes a good citizen?
options: A. A good citizen is mainly someone who avoids breaking laws
B. A good citizen is someone who volunteers and helps within the community
C. A good citizen is someone who only follows legal rules without extra involvement
D. A good citizen is defined mainly by their political beliefs

multipleChoice.question: What is the main idea expressed by Myra Anubi about good citizenship?
options: A. Paying taxes is the most important duty of a citizen
B. Good citizens should improve their community beyond obeying laws
C. Citizenship only requires obeying existing laws and regulations
D. Voting is the only way to show good citizenship

multipleChoice.question: What does the term ‘law of the land’ mean as explained in the programme?
options: A. A system of rules applied only in rural areas
B. The collection of laws and rules valid in a country
C. Laws that change based on local government decisions
D. Laws specifically related to land ownership

multipleChoice.question: What problem regarding young people and politics does Neil highlight?
options: A. Young people are more likely to participate in elections
B. Young people feel disconnected and ignored by politicians
C. Politicians rely heavily on youth votes for decisions
D. Young people are well informed but uninterested in voting

multipleChoice.question: The name ‘MyPolis’ is derived from an Ancient Greek word. What does ‘polis’ actually mean?
options: A. Vote
B. People
C. City
D. Government

multipleChoice.question: According to Bernardo Goncalves, what issue does MyPolis aim to address?
options: A. Increasing trust between citizens and political institutions
B. Making parliament sessions more entertaining for youth
C. Encouraging young people to form political parties
D. Reducing taxes to involve young citizens in democracy

multipleChoice.question: What does ‘erosion of trust’ mean in the context of this programme?
options: A. Sudden loss of trust caused by political scandals
B. Gradual weakening of confidence in political institutions
C. Complete loss of trust in government due to war
D. Temporary decrease in trust during election campaigns

multipleChoice.question: How is MyPolis incorporated into schools, as Alison Roberts mentions?
options: A. As a compulsory subject replacing traditional classes
B. As an optional after-school activity unrelated to curriculum
C. As a part of the national curriculum from the start
D. As a one-time event to raise awareness about citizenship

multipleChoice.question: What impressed Myra Anubi most about the MyPolis programme?
options: A. Politicians listen seriously to children and respond to their ideas
B. Children always succeed in changing school policies
C. Politicians rarely attend school council meetings
D. The programme is run solely by government officials

multipleChoice.question: What does the phrase “hard to pin down” mean as used in the programme?
options: A. Difficult to force someone to reveal their true intentions
B. Easy to contact politicians and get clear answers
C. Physically unable to catch or hold someone
D. Someone who is frequently absent from public events
